blockingIssues.description: DefaultPolicyDropdown ownership cleanup remains snapshot-lag dependent, so a user-authored override can still be unset by a quick default switch before runtime_status reflects the user's update.
policy-denial-roundtrip still does not enforce the 15s dispatch-to-failure bound in the requestId-null branch because it may wait up to 30s for request id discovery before starting the 15s timer.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make policy-direction semantics unambiguous across all policy surfaces by explicitly documenting PeerPolicyChip direction as implemented (or requiring a follow-up alignment change), and keep mission guidance consistent with code comments/tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both fix-m3-default-policy-direction-respond and fix-m3-default-policy-no-clobber-user-overrides reviews observed AGENTS guidance stating chips target respond.* while implementation still defaults chip writes to request.*.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify ui-screen-worker/runtime-worker agent-browser requirements for logic-only or e2e-spec-only fixes so workers can remain compliant without reporting procedural deviations when no UI-surface verification is applicable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple fix reviews reported followedProcedure=false solely because work was non-visual (state-invariant or spec-hardening) while skill text still implied mandatory agent-browser validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify denial-roundtrip evidence language to distinguish strict timing from dispatch time and to explicitly call out when Sign-typed pending invariance is the required observable versus total pending queue length.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-m3-denial-roundtrip-15s-bound-and-a-side-invariance review noted contract/evidence wording drift versus implemented and runtime-realistic assertions.

rejectedObservations.observation: No demo scenario exists for the exact runtime offline-peer/permissionState combination used by the PeerRow parity fix.
Repeated policy-direction mismatch observations from two fix reviews.
Repeated skill strictness observations for non-visual fixes from multiple fix reviews.
library/architecture.md denial-event wording drift was flagged, but not updated in this scrutiny run.
